text.title: Cloud LB Terraform Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when provisioning cloud LBs via Terraform. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Engineer ran `terraform destroy` in the wrong workspace; the prod ALB and its target groups disappeared. Service is down; restoring requires manual recreation + DNS update.
antipattern.root-cause: `enable_deletion_protection = true` was missing on the prod `aws_lb` resource.
antipattern.fix: Set `enable_deletion_protection = true` on every production LB. Add `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` as a belt-and-braces. Block `terraform destroy` on prod via OPA / Terraform Cloud policies.

antipattern.symptom: External PCI-DSS audit fails: scanner reports the LB accepts TLS 1.0 and supports CBC ciphers. Mitigation deadline is 30 days.

root-cause.source: Mozilla TLS guidelines + AWS ELB security policies

antipattern.root-cause: `ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-2016-08"` (default suggested by old AWS docs) was copy-pasted into the Terraform.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06` (AWS) or `MODERN` (GCP). Run `nmap --script ssl-enum-ciphers -p 443` after apply to confirm.

antipattern.symptom: Honeypot detects scans hitting backend EC2 instances directly on port 8080, bypassing the ALB and WAF. Logs show the SG allows `0.0.0.0/0` on the backend port.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied a wide SG rule (`cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]`) onto the backend instead of referencing the ALB SG ID.
antipattern.fix: Backend SG ingress MUST use `source_security_group_id = aws_security_group.alb.id`, never a CIDR. tflint rule `aws_security_group_invalid_ingress` enforces this in CI.
